response: The internet's history began in the 1960s with U.S. Department of Defense-funded ARPANET, which was demonstrated in 1969, and grew through the 1970s; the 1983 transition to TCP/IP protocols laid the foundation for the modern internet, followed by Tim Berners-Lee's 1989 invention of the World Wide Web (with HTML, URLs, and HTTP) and the 1993 launch of Mosaic, which made the web accessible to ordinary users. The late 1990s saw a dot-com boom with massive investment in internet companies, many of which failed in the 2001 crash, though survivors like Amazon and Google became some of the most valuable companies; the mid-2000s brought social media (Facebook, Twitter, YouTube), transforming communication, while the 2007 iPhone launch spurred mobile internet, and 2006's AWS kickstarted cloud computing. Today, the internet connects over 5 billion people and underpins nearly all aspects of modern life, from commerce to communication to entertainment.
